name: p0-garage-door-partial-spring
description: My garage door opens about a foot then stops, and it won't close at all. One torsion spring above the door is missing — there's just a broken coil of metal hanging where it should be. The other spring on the opposite side still looks intact. No photo attached.

hypotheses.label: Broken torsion spring
evidence_for: User reports one spring is visibly broken or missing
Door opens partially then stops and cannot close — classic loss of counterbalance
Torsion springs are always paired; one absent means both must be replaced

hypotheses.label: Snapped lifting cable
evidence_for: Failure to close can also be caused by cable failure
evidence_against: User specifically described a missing spring, not a slack cable

hypotheses.label: Opener motor over-current cutout
evidence_for: Partial-open-then-stop can be a motor overload trip
evidence_against: User reports a visible mechanical failure of the spring, not an electrical symptom

mockAgentOutput.what_we_dont_know: Whether the lifting cable has also been damaged by the spring failure — affects whether this is a spring-only or spring-plus-cable replacement.
mockAgentOutput.why_it_matters: Replacing only the springs when the cable is also damaged leads to a callback; knowing both lets the contractor quote and order parts correctly the first time.

chips.text: Cables look slack on at least one side.
Cables look tight, only the spring is broken.
Door makes a humming or grinding sound.

explicit_unknowns: whether the lifting cable is also damaged
whether the opener motor still functions

expected.must_ask_about: whether the lifting cable is also damaged

failure_baseline_today.observed_behaviour: System returns Security / Garage Door with confidence ~78 and title 'Unclear — More Detail Needed'. After the user supplies more detail, the same clarification chips return — the doom loop.
failure_baseline_today.why_it_fails: Single-integer confidence is conservative when no image is present, even though the verbal description uniquely identifies the failed component. The prompt's worked examples are about visual ambiguity and user corrections; there is no rubric anchor for 'confident from a complete text description alone'.

expected.phase_6_commit_rationale: Top hypothesis confidence_alone=0.92 (>=0.75), gap to second=0.57 (>=0.25), evidence_for has 3 entries, evidence_against empty. Component named directly in description so Phase 4 component_confidence will be >=85 despite image_sufficiency=absent.
